perturbation.original_text: "Licensed Content" shall mean all Content (i) whose copyright is owned by Licensor; or (ii) owned by a third party provider but is sublicensable by Licensor to Licensee without requiring the payment of any additional fee to any third party and without violating the terms of any agreement with such third party provider, together with all updates to and substitutions therefor as may be implemented by Licensor or such third party provider.
perturbation.changed_text: "Licensed Content" shall mean all Content (i) whose copyright is owned by Licensor; or (ii) owned by a third party provider but is sublicensable by Licensor to Licensee, at Licensor's sole discretion, and Licensor can revoke such permission at any time without violating the terms of any agreement with such third party provider, together with all updates to and substitutions therefor as may be implemented by Licensor or such third party provider.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Licensed Content' implies an assured right for Licensee to use sublicensable content from third-party providers. The modified definition introduces Licensor's sole discretion and revocation power, creating a contradiction. This is because the original definition does not entail Licensor having the ability to take back what has been sublicensed, while the updated version entails Licensor having full control on what gets sublicensed, which may conflict with the original definition of the term Licensed Content. The contradiction manifests in Section 3.2, where the Licensee is expected to use Licensed Domain Names in accordance with content distribution policies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2. Content Distribution. Licensor shall make available to Licensee the Licensed Content in substantially the same manner and with substantially the same speed and efficiency as such Licensed Content was made available to SINA Leju prior to the Effective Date, namely through Licensor's content database, but in no event with less speed, efficiency, or a lesser level of access than Licensor provides with respect to its own operations. Licensee agrees to use the Licensed Domain Names only in accordance with such content distribution policy that Licensor uses in connection with its own business, and as may be established by Licensor and communicated in writing in advance to Licensee from time to time or as may otherwise be agreed to by the Parties from time to time, provided that Licensee shall be afforded the same period of time to implement any such content distribution policy as is afforded to Licensor's Affiliates and other third parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Licensed Content' introduces Licensor's sole discretion and revocation power over third-party content that was initially presented as assured to the Licensee in the original definition. This directly contradicts Section 3.2, which expects the Licensee to use the Licensed Content and Licensed Domain Names in accordance with established policies. The Licensor's new ability to revoke access to content undermines the Licensee's ability to reliably fulfill its obligations under Section 3.2. The original text ensures third-party content is 'sublicensable by Licensor to Licensee without requiring the payment of any additional fee to any third party', whereas the changed text adds 'at Licensor's sole discretion, and Licensor can revoke such permission at any time', creating a clear contradiction in terms.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2. Prosecution and Maintenance. As between Licensee and Licensor, Licensor shall have the sole and exclusive right and obligation to maintain and renew registrations for the Licensed Domain Names during the Term, and shall do so at its own cost and expense during the Term. Licensee shall not engage in the foregoing affairs, in particular, Licensee shall not change or apply for change of the domain name registration service agency for the Licensed Domain Names during the Term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2. Prosecution and Maintenance. As between Licensee and Licensor, Licensee shall have the sole and exclusive right and obligation to maintain and renew registrations for the Licensed Domain Names during the Term, and shall do so at its own cost and expense during the Term. Licensor shall not engage in the foregoing affairs, in particular, Licensor shall not change or apply for change of the domain name registration service agency for the Licensed Domain Names during the Term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the Licensor is responsible for the domain name registration. By switching Licensee and Licensor, the text now implies that Licensee is responsible for domain names, while the Licensor may not engage in that afffair. This contradicts the statements made in Section 4.1, which grants Licensor ownership of domain names.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Ownership. Licensee acknowledges that, as between the Parties, Licensor (or its third party providers) is the owner of all right, title and interest in and to the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content, and all such right, title and interest shall remain exclusively with Licensor (or its third party providers).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts section 4.1. Section 4.2 originally states that the Licensor is responsible for maintaining the domain names. The change shifts this responsibility to the Licensee. Section 4.1 clearly grants ownership of the domain names to the Licensor. Granting the Licensee responsibility for maintaining the *Licensor's* domain names creates a contradiction. The change introduces a conflict regarding who is responsible for the domain names, which are owned by the Licensor, and therefore a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 10.7. Assignment. This Agreement and any rights or authority granted hereunder shall not be assigned or transferred by either Party, including by operation of law, merger or otherwise, without the express written consent of the other Party, provided that Licensor may assign this Agreement without consent to any of its Affiliates and Licensee may assign this Agreement without consent to SINA Leju or an Affiliate of Licensee that is controlled by SINA Leju.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.7. Assignment. This Agreement and any rights or authority granted hereunder shall not be assigned or transferred by either Party, including by operation of law, merger or otherwise, without the express written consent of the other Party, provided that Licensor must get written consent to assign this Agreement to any of its Affiliates and Licensee may assign this Agreement without consent to SINA Leju or an Affiliate of Licensee that is controlled by SINA Leju.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows Licensor to assign this agreement without consent to its affiliates. The modified version enforces that Licensor must get written consent to assign this agreement to its affiliates. This introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 8.1 where Licensor is obligated to indemnify Licensee, but if the agreement is assigned without consent, the indemnification clause is now dubious and could make it difficult for Licensee to seek indemnification in case of breaches or issues arising after such an unapproved assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1. Indemnification by Licensor. Licensor shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensee and its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, shareholders, successors and assigns, (collectively, the "Licensee Parties") from and against any claim, suit, demand or action ("Action"), and any and all direct losses suffered or incurred by Licensee in connection with any third party claims arising out of or resulting from any breach by Licensor of any provision of this Agreement. Licensor's obligation to indemnify Licensee shall be conditioned on (a) Licensee's provision to Licensor of prompt notice of such an Action (except where any delay does not materially prejudice Licensor); (b) Licensee's reasonable cooperation with Licensor in the defense and settlement of such an Action at Licensor's cost; and (c) Licensor having exclusive control of the defense, settlement and/or compromise of such an Action (provided that Licensor may not settle any Action in a manner that adversely affects Licensee without Licensee's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed).
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Licensor to assign the agreement to its affiliates without consent. The changed text now requires Licensor to obtain written consent to assign the agreement to its affiliates. This directly contradicts the indemnification clause in Section 8.1, because if Licensor assigns the agreement without the Licensee's consent (which is now required where it wasn't before), the enforceability of the indemnification obligation becomes dubious, creating a direct conflict between the newly changed assignment clause and the existing indemnification clause.
